title: Conform & validate
text: artoo’s core rule operation silently damages data. coercion truncate fractions overflow R’s 32-bit integer range aborts artoo_error_type touching value — gate independent conformance, turning conformance bypass : two honest one-line exits: keep wider source type apply_spec(on_coercion_loss = "keep") (value preserved mismatch left integer_fraction finding), retype spec set_type().
code: vars <- spec_variables(adam_spec) vars$data_type[vars$variable == "AGE"] <- "integer" strict <- artoo_spec(   adam_spec@datasets, vars,   codelists = adam_spec@codelists,   study = spec_study(adam_spec) ) raw <- cdisc_adsl raw$AGE[1] <- raw$AGE[1] + 0.5 apply_spec(raw, strict, "ADSL", conformance = "off") Error: ! Coercion to the spec dataTypes would lose data. ✖ Integer coercion would truncate fractional values in: AGE (1). ℹ This gate is separate from `conformance`; `conformance = "off"` does not   bypass it. ℹ To keep these values in R, set `apply_spec(on_coercion_loss = "keep")`, or   retype the spec with `set_type()` (dataType "float" or "decimal"). ℹ To see every finding at once, run `check_spec(x, spec, dataset)`.

title: Migrating clinical data from WLATIN1 to UTF-8
text: read_xpt() detects encoding: every byte file validates UTF-8 reads UTF-8, otherwise assumes Windows-1252 — right default legacy clinical files. Formats whose spec pins UTF-8 (Dataset-JSON, Parquet) read UTF-8 refuse loudly bytes disagree, rather guessing: refusal prompt name true source charset. encoding= transcode, label — bytes converted UTF-8 normalised (NFC), lands R ordinary text: SAS IANA spelling works — "wlatin1", "cp1252", "windows-1252" charset; OEM/DOS names ("pcoem850") old archive might declare. rosetta artoo_encodings(). Note "latin1" (ISO-8859-1) WLATIN1: two differ across 0x80–0x9F range holds Euro sign every smart quote.

text: classic silent corruption reading WLATIN1 bytes UTF-8 (SAS terms: data set whose encoding attribute lies). bytes validate UTF-8, invalid_encoding conformance dimension flags writer runs: fix always upstream — re-read source true encoding= — never re-label.
code: bad <- cdisc_dm bad$ETHNIC <- rawToChar(as.raw(c(0x4D, 0xDC, 0x4E))) # WLATIN1 bytes, raw f <- check_spec(bad, sdtm_spec, "DM") f[f$check == "invalid_encoding", c("variable", "severity", "message")] variable severity 14   ETHNIC    error                                                                                                 message 14 'ETHNIC' has 60 value(s) whose bytes are not valid UTF-8; re-read the source with its true encoding.

what: 3. Lengths are bytes, and UTF-8 needs more of them
title: Migrating clinical data from WLATIN1 to UTF-8
text: WLATIN1 character always one byte; UTF-8 character can take four. spec length calibrated WLATIN1 data (Ö = 1 byte) -declares UTF-8 form (Ö = 2 bytes). SAS handles CVP engine guessed multiplier; artoo measures actual bytes, never truncates, tells declared length give: length_overflow check reports fact conformance time, migration can fix spec (durable answer) instead relying writer’s defence.
code: sp <- artoo_spec(   data.frame(dataset = "DM"),   data.frame(     dataset = "DM", variable = "INVNAM",     data_type = "string", length = 6L   ) ) x <- apply_spec(   data.frame(INVNAM = "Öztürk"), sp, "DM",   conformance = "off" ) xpt <- tempfile(fileext = ".xpt") write_xpt(x, xpt) Warning in write_xpt(x, xpt): Widened 1 column past the declared spec length: "INVNAM (6 -> 8)". ℹ Values need more bytes than the spec length; data was kept whole. ℹ Update the spec length, or shorten the data, so the file matches its declared   metadata.

what: 4. Smart punctuation: the WLATIN1 characters ASCII can fold
title: Migrating clinical data from WLATIN1 to UTF-8
text: Text passed word processor carries typographic punctuation — curly quotes, en/em dashes, ellipses. WLATIN1 live rows 8–9 code page (one byte ); UTF-8 three bytes ; US-ASCII exist . also one class character exact ASCII equivalent, published SAS NLS punctuation fold: Every writer accepts on_invalid = "translit", applies exactly table nothing else: "translit" deliberately narrower "replace": punctuation folds ASCII form carries meaning; character equivalent — diacritic name — still aborts, "Öztürk" silently becoming "Ozturk" (worse, "?zt?rk") data corruption, conversion:
code: note <- data.frame(   USUBJID = "01-701-1015",   COMMENT = "Patient’s dose – “held”…" ) ascii_xpt <- tempfile(fileext = ".xpt") write_xpt(note, ascii_xpt, encoding = "US-ASCII", on_invalid = "translit") Warning in write_xpt(note, ascii_xpt, encoding = "US-ASCII", on_invalid = "translit"): Transliterated smart punctuation to ASCII in 1 value for "US-ASCII". ℹ The fold follows the SAS NLS punctuation table (quotes, dashes, ellipsis,   bullet). read_xpt(ascii_xpt)$COMMENT [1] "Patient's dose - \"held\"..." name <- data.frame(INVNAM = "Öztürk") write_xpt(name, tempfile(fileext = ".xpt"),   encoding = "US-ASCII", on_invalid = "translit" ) Error in `write_xpt()`: ! Cannot encode 1 value to "US-ASCII" even after punctuation folding. ✖ Offending value: "Öztürk". ℹ Only smart punctuation has an ASCII fold; use `on_invalid = "fold"` to also   strip accents, or write to Dataset-JSON (UTF-8).

what: 5. Accent stripping: the full fold, opted into
title: Migrating clinical data from WLATIN1 to UTF-8
text: Sometimes stripping accents documented migration decision — SAS BASECHAR() function exists exactly . artoo’s equivalent on_invalid = "fold": punctuation table plus ICU Latin-ASCII transliteration rest WLATIN1 range (À–ÿ base letters, Æ → AE, ß → ss, Þ → TH, × → *, «» → << >>, ½ → 1/2). table pinned inside artoo, — unlike iconv //TRANSLIT, whose output differs Linux macOS — fold byte-identical every platform: warning audit trail: fold one-way, original characters recoverable output. character neither authority maps ASCII — Euro sign, trademark sign, µ, ° — still aborts, artoo invents mappings: escalation ladder, least lossy: "error" (refuse) → "translit" (fold punctuation ) → "fold" (also strip accents) → "replace" (? per character) → "ignore" (drop). Pick earliest rung migration plan allows.
code: names_df <- data.frame(INVNAM = c("Öztürk", "Straße", "Ærø")) folded_xpt <- tempfile(fileext = ".xpt") write_xpt(names_df, folded_xpt, encoding = "US-ASCII", on_invalid = "fold") Warning in write_xpt(names_df, folded_xpt, encoding = "US-ASCII", on_invalid = "fold"): Folded 3 values to ASCII for "US-ASCII" (accents stripped). ℹ The fold follows the SAS NLS punctuation table plus ICU Latin-ASCII; the   original characters are not recoverable from the output. read_xpt(folded_xpt)$INVNAM [1] "Ozturk"  "Strasse" "AEro" cost <- data.frame(COMMENT = "total €100") write_xpt(cost, tempfile(fileext = ".xpt"),   encoding = "US-ASCII", on_invalid = "fold" ) Error in `write_xpt()`: ! Cannot encode 1 value to "US-ASCII" even after ASCII folding. ✖ Offending value: "total €100". ℹ The character has no standards-backed ASCII fold (ICU Latin-ASCII leaves it   unmapped); write to Dataset-JSON (UTF-8), or set `on_invalid`.

title: Migrating clinical data from WLATIN1 to UTF-8
text: Read true source encoding. Let read_xpt() detect, pass encoding = "wlatin1" explicitly formats carry answer. Verify check_spec() — zero invalid_encoding findings means bytes arrived characters. Re-measure lengths bytes. Run length_overflow dimension UTF-8 form update spec multibyte characters grew. Write UTF-8 default (Dataset-JSON, Parquet, xpt v8 carry losslessly). US-ASCII submission XPT, write encoding = "US-ASCII", on_invalid = "translit" — punctuation folds, genuine data problems stay loud. Escalate on_invalid = "fold" accent stripping documented step migration plan.

title: Recipes
text: Clinical dates travel two shapes, artoo keeps distinction explicit dataType, targetDataType, displayFormat. Scope note: carriage — partial-date imputation analysis decision SAP owns; artoo never imputes. SDTM --DTC variable typed date ISO 8601 text CDISC storage rule, stays character pipeline, partial values included ("2024-03" legal ISO date, padding imputation stealth): variable typed date targetDataType realizes R Date memory: ADaM numeric-date convention shape: variable carried integer SAS-epoch day count, displayFormat (date9.) telling SAS render . TRTSDT one, format rides metadata: SAS TIME values import hms (seconds since midnight), values past 24 hours, negative values, fractional seconds survive: Either date shape round-trips byte-faithfully — SAS-epoch (1960-01-01) versus R-epoch conversion happens inside codecs, never code:
code: dm <- apply_spec(cdisc_dm, sdtm_spec, "DM", conformance = "off") class(dm$RFSTDTC) [1] "character" adsl <- apply_spec(cdisc_adsl, adam_spec, "ADSL", conformance = "off") class(adsl$DISONSDT) [1] "Date" class(adsl$TRTSDT) [1] "integer" p <- tempfile(fileext = ".json") write_json(adsl, p) get_meta(read_json(p))@columns$TRTSDT[c("dataType", "displayFormat")] $dataType [1] "integer"  $displayFormat [1] "date9." hms::as_hms(30615) 08:30:15 identical(read_json(p)$TRTSDT, adsl$TRTSDT) [1] TRUE

text: Ordered pipeline. Four fixed steps run order: coerce column CDISC dataType, reorder columns spec, sort rows dataset keys, stamp metadata. spec variable data lacks never fabricated empty column: artoo lossless carrier, deriver. reported instead, informational heads-apply time plus missing_variable finding (mandatory) missing_permissible (), left absent, conformed frame carries columns data actually . Keeping default artoo lossless construction: metadata-application step silently discarded columns break contract, trimming data always explicit, announced choice rather default side effect. extra = "drop" opts trim--spec (returned frame carries exactly spec's columns): undeclared columns removed check, findings describe exactly returned frame (dropped column never reported extra_variable), drop always announced (artoo_message_apply) audit trail removed — even conformance = "". Lossless abort, call. coercion damage values — integer dataType truncating fractions overflowing R's 32-bit range — aborts artoo_error_type value touched, default on_coercion_loss = "error". gate independent conformance: conformance = "" bypass . data (spec) right, set on_coercion_loss = "keep": column keeps wider source type divergence reported integer_fraction / integer_overflow finding, never silently truncated. spec wrong, retype set_type() (repair_spec() findings). error abort carries offending rows data: cnd$variables data frame columns variable, data_type, n, reason ("truncated" / "overflowed"), pipeline can collect every mismatch one tryCatch(..., artoo_error_type = function(cnd) cnd$variables) pass. Coded variables keep submission values (SEX stays "M"); codelist translation verb, decode_column().

title: Conform a data frame to its spec — apply_spec
code: # ---- Example 1: conform ADSL, then read its metadata ---- # # The bundled adam_spec describes ADSL; the raw frame is coerced, # ordered, sorted, and stamped with the CDISC metadata get_meta() reads # back. Variables the spec declares but this extract never derived are # reported (not added), readable via conformance(). adsl <- apply_spec(cdisc_adsl, adam_spec, "ADSL") #> 6 variables the spec declares are absent from the data (not added): #> `TRTDURD`, `DISONDT`, `EOSSTT`, `DCSREAS`, `EOSDISP`, and `MMS1TSBL`. #> ℹ See `conformance(x)` for the findings. get_meta(adsl)@dataset$records #> [1] 60  # ---- Example 2: extras are kept and reported, or dropped on request ---- # # By default a column outside the spec rides along (reported by the # extra_variable finding) and still writes losslessly; extra = "drop" # trims to the spec, announced and still reported. DM is SDTM, so it # conforms against the bundled sdtm_spec. raw <- cdisc_dm raw$DERIVED <- seq_len(nrow(raw)) dm <- apply_spec(raw, sdtm_spec, "DM") #> 1 variable the spec declares is absent from the data (not added): #> `BRTHDTC`. #> ℹ See `conformance(x)` for the findings. findings <- conformance(dm) findings[findings$check == "extra_variable", c("variable", "message")] #>    variable                                        message #> 2  RFXSTDTC Column 'RFXSTDTC' is not declared in the spec. #> 3  RFXENDTC Column 'RFXENDTC' is not declared in the spec. #> 4   RFICDTC  Column 'RFICDTC' is not declared in the spec. #> 5  RFPENDTC Column 'RFPENDTC' is not declared in the spec. #> 6    DTHDTC   Column 'DTHDTC' is not declared in the spec. #> 7     DTHFL    Column 'DTHFL' is not declared in the spec. #> 8  ACTARMCD Column 'ACTARMCD' is not declared in the spec. #> 9    ACTARM   Column 'ACTARM' is not declared in the spec. #> 10    DMDTC    Column 'DMDTC' is not declared in the spec. #> 11     DMDY     Column 'DMDY' is not declared in the spec. #> 12  DERIVED  Column 'DERIVED' is not declared in the spec. trimmed <- apply_spec(raw, sdtm_spec, "DM", extra = "drop") #> 1 variable the spec declares is absent from the data (not added): #> `BRTHDTC`. #> ℹ See `conformance(x)` for the findings. #> Dropped 11 undeclared variables: `RFXSTDTC`, `RFXENDTC`, `RFICDTC`, #> `RFPENDTC`, `DTHDTC`, `DTHFL`, `ACTARMCD`, `ACTARM`, `DMDTC`, `DMDY`, #> and `DERIVED` "DERIVED" %in% names(trimmed) #> [1] FALSE

what: artoo: Lossless CDISC-Native Input and Output for Clinical Datasets — artoo-package
title: artoo: Lossless CDISC-Native Input and Output for Clinical Datasets — artoo-package
text: Reads writes clinical-trial datasets losslessly across 'SAS' XPORT (XPT), Clinical Data Interchange Standards Consortium (CDISC) Dataset-JSON, 'Apache Parquet', applying specification produce submission-ready Study Data Tabulation Model (SDTM) Analysis Data Model (ADaM) datasets. single canonical metadata model carries labels, CDISC data types, lengths, 'SAS' display formats, controlled-terminology references, sort keys identically across every format, conversion two formats lossless construction. Pure 'R' lightweight, external 'SAS' 'Java' runtime. Implements published format specifications CDISC Dataset-JSON (https://cdisc-org.github.io/DataExchange-DatasetJson/doc/dataset-json1-1.html) 'SAS' XPORT (https://www.loc.gov/preservation/digital/formats/fdd/fdd000466.shtml).

title: Control which conformance checks run — artoo_checks
text: missing_variable Flag mandatory spec variables absent data. <logical(1)>: default TRUE. missing_permissible Flag permissible (non-mandatory) spec variables absent data. <logical(1)>: default TRUE. extra_variable Flag data columns spec declare. <logical(1)>: default TRUE. type_mismatch Flag columns whose storage differs spec dataType. <logical(1)>: default TRUE. invalid_encoding Flag character values whose bytes valid UTF-8. <logical(1)>: default TRUE. Invalid bytes mean source read mis-declared encoding; re-read true encoding= corruption reaches writer. length_overflow Flag character values longer spec length. <logical(1)>: default TRUE. char_length_limit Flag character values longer SAS XPORT v5 / FDA 200-byte limit. <logical(1)>: default TRUE. codelist_membership Flag values outside closed codelist. <logical(1)>: default TRUE. codelist_membership_extensible Flag values outside extensible codelist's enumerated terms. <logical(1)>: default TRUE. codelist whose extended flag TRUE allows sponsor terms, non-member note, never error; toggle silences notes independently codelist_membership. label_match Flag column whose label attribute differs spec label. <logical(1)>: default TRUE. key_uniqueness Flag dataset whose spec key variables uniquely identify rows. <logical(1)>: default TRUE. display_format Flag date/datetime/time variable whose displayFormat recognized SAS format family. <logical(1)>: default TRUE. variable_name Flag data column name violates XPORT naming rules. <logical(1)>: default TRUE. 8 characters (v5 limit), 32 (v8 limit), containing anything ASCII letters, digits, underscore. dataset_name Flag dataset name violates XPORT naming rules. <logical(1)>: default TRUE. limits variable_name. label_length Flag column label attribute 40-byte XPORT v5 / FDA limit. <logical(1)>: default TRUE. integer_overflow Flag integer-typed variable holding values beyond R's 32-bit integer range. <logical(1)>: default TRUE. values become NA coercion, error, warning. integer_fraction Flag integer-typed variable holding fractional values. <logical(1)>: default TRUE. Coercion truncate (162.6 becomes 162) — data-integrity event; fix spec dataType (float / decimal) data conforming. iso8601_format Flag character date/datetime/time variable whose values valid ISO 8601 text. <logical(1)>: default TRUE. character column temporal dataType CDISC --DTC form; complete values, right-truncated partials ("1951", "1951-12"), SDTMIG hyphen placeholders ("2003---15") pass, "12NOV2019" impossible calendar date flagged.

title: Construct a CDISC specification — artoo_spec
text: datasets Dataset-level metadata table. <data.frame>: required. One row per dataset; must carry dataset column. Optional columns label, class, structure, keys filled NA absent. variables Variable-level metadata table. <data.frame>: required. One row per variable; must carry dataset, variable, data_type. data_type column canonicalised CDISC dataType (e.g. "text" becomes "string"). Requirement: every dataset value must appear datasets. codelists Controlled-terminology terms. <data.frame> | NULL. Must carry codelist_id term supplied. Interaction: every codelist_id referenced variables must resolve . study Study-level metadata. <data.frame> | NULL. single row named study fields. Well-known fields canonicalised study_name, study_description, protocol_name (aliases StudyName studyid resolve automatically); fields pass verbatim. standard field, present, consumed @standard. values Value-level (VLM) metadata. <data.frame> | NULL. methods Derivation methods. <data.frame> | NULL. Define-XML method definitions variables reference method_id; must carry method_id supplied. Completeness (e.g. referenced method description) checked validate_spec(), . comments Comment definitions. <data.frame> | NULL. Referenced comment_id; must carry comment_id supplied. documents Document references. <data.frame> | NULL. Referenced document_id; must carry document_id supplied. standard CDISC standard spec implements. <character(1)> | NULL. E.g. "ADaMIG 1.1" "SDTMIG 3.2". NULL (default) resolved datasets$standard study$standard; absent everywhere, @standard NA. Restriction: sources must agree one value; conflicting standards abort artoo_error_spec.

title: Construct a CDISC specification — artoo_spec
text: validated artoo_spec object. Inspect spec_datasets() / spec_variables(), check validate_spec().

title: Construct a CDISC specification — artoo_spec
text: Coerce, validate. table first coerced plain data frame (tibble accepted demoted); known columns cast storage mode absent optional columns added typed NA, every downstream reader can trust schema. Validation runs coercion, completed slots. Type canonicalisation. variables$data_type mapped closed CDISC dataType vocabulary (string, integer, decimal, float, double, boolean, date, datetime, time, URI). Common SAS / P21 spellings resolve automatically ("text", "Char", "integer (8)", ...); unrecognised token aborts artoo_error_type. Cross-slot integrity. Construction fails (artoo_error_spec) variable names dataset absent datasets, references codelist_id absent codelists. One spec, one standard. artoo_spec carries exactly one CDISC standard, stored scalar @standard property. constructor resolves standard argument, standard column datasets (P21 workbook shape), standard field study (Define-XML shape) — columns consumed, @standard single home. one distinct value aborts artoo_error_spec; scope source one standard (e.g. read_spec(path, datasets = ...)) instead mixing. One study vocabulary. Well-known study fields canonicalised CDISC ODM GlobalVariables names, snake_cased: study_name, study_description, protocol_name. Source spellings resolve automatically (StudyName, studyid, ...); fields vocabulary know pass verbatim. Aliases disagree value abort artoo_error_spec.

title: Construct a CDISC specification — artoo_spec
code: # ---- Example 1: build a spec from the bundled CDISC-pilot tables ---- # # `cdisc_sdtm_datasets` and `cdisc_sdtm_variables` hold the CDISC pilot SDTM # metadata in the shape artoo_spec() expects; the constructor # canonicalises every type and checks cross-slot integrity. spec <- artoo_spec(cdisc_sdtm_datasets, cdisc_sdtm_variables, codelists = cdisc_codelists) spec_datasets(spec) #> [1] "DM"  # ---- Example 2: a focused spec for a single dataset ---- # # Slice the bundled tables to one dataset (DM) to build a smaller spec. dm_ds <- cdisc_sdtm_datasets[cdisc_sdtm_datasets$dataset == "DM", ] dm_var <- cdisc_sdtm_variables[cdisc_sdtm_variables$dataset == "DM", ] dm_spec <- artoo_spec(dm_ds, dm_var, codelists = cdisc_codelists) head(spec_variables(dm_spec, "DM")[, c("variable", "label", "data_type")]) #>   variable                             label data_type #> 1  STUDYID                  Study Identifier    string #> 2   DOMAIN               Domain Abbreviation    string #> 3  USUBJID         Unique Subject Identifier    string #> 4   SUBJID  Subject Identifier for the Study    string #> 5  RFSTDTC Subject Reference Start Date/Time    string #> 6  RFENDTC   Subject Reference End Date/Time    string

text: Findings, enforcement. check_spec() never modifies data; returns every divergence finds. apply_spec() runs decides via conformance argument (warn, abort, ). dimensions checked : missing variables (split mandatory, error, permissible, warning), extra variables (data column spec declare), type mismatch, ISO 8601 validity character date/datetime/time values (CDISC partials pass; "12NOV2019" ), fractional values 32-bit overflow integer dataType (corrupt data coercion), character length overflow, hard 200-byte XPORT v5 / FDA character limit, codelist membership, label drift spec, key uniqueness, displayFormat validity. Decode-aware membership. decode selects codelist column data checked , matching apply_spec()'s decode step: "none"/"to_code" check codelist terms, "to_decode" decodes. apply_spec() threads decode , decoded column wrongly flagged. Fatal vs informational coercion checks. integer_fraction integer_overflow carry error severity: mark data integer dataType hold without loss, apply_spec() refuses coerce (on_coercion_loss governs gate). type_mismatch note: column stored widely spec declares (integer-valued double, instance) coerces cleanly, informational, blocker.

title: Check a dataset against its spec — check_spec
code: # ---- Example 1: a conformed frame surfaces only the genuine gaps ---- # # apply_spec() coerces and orders to spec but never fabricates a variable # the data lacks; checking the result reports the permissible variables # this extract never derived (here, six) instead of hiding them as empty # columns. adsl <- apply_spec(cdisc_adsl, adam_spec, "ADSL", conformance = "off") #> 6 variables the spec declares are absent from the data (not added): #> `TRTDURD`, `DISONDT`, `EOSSTT`, `DCSREAS`, `EOSDISP`, and `MMS1TSBL`. nrow(check_spec(adsl, adam_spec, "ADSL")) #> [1] 12  # ---- Example 2: raw data surfaces divergences ---- # # Checking a raw frame with an undeclared column flags the extras. raw <- cdisc_adsl raw$NOTASPEC <- 1 head(check_spec(raw, adam_spec, "ADSL")[, c("check", "variable", "severity")]) #>                 check variable severity #> 1 missing_permissible  TRTDURD  warning #> 2 missing_permissible  DISONDT  warning #> 3 missing_permissible   EOSSTT  warning #> 4 missing_permissible  DCSREAS  warning #> 5 missing_permissible  EOSDISP  warning #> 6 missing_permissible MMS1TSBL  warning

code: # ---- Example 1: scan a whole study in one pass ---- # # Loop the conformance check over every dataset's data. A fractional AGE # (the spec types it integer) surfaces as an integer_fraction finding; the # print is a dataset-by-check count matrix. adsl <- cdisc_adsl adsl$AGE <- adsl$AGE + 0.5 check_study(adam_spec, list(ADSL = adsl, ADAE = cdisc_adae)) #> <artoo_study_findings> 2 datasets: 1 error, 11 warnings, 39 notes #>  #>      codelist_membership_extensible extra_variable integer_fraction #> ADAE                              0              0                0 #> ADSL                              1              5                1 #>      label_match missing_permissible type_mismatch #> ADAE           7                   0            17 #> ADSL           3                   6            11 #>  #> i Treat this as a findings frame: filter by severity, or pass it to repair_spec().  # ---- Example 2: feed the findings straight into repair_spec() ---- # # The result is an ordinary findings frame, so repair_spec() consumes it to # flip every integer_fraction / integer_overflow variable across the study. findings <- check_study(adam_spec, list(ADSL = adsl)) fixed <- repair_spec(adam_spec, findings) spec_variables(fixed, "ADSL")$data_type[   spec_variables(fixed, "ADSL")$variable == "AGE" ] #> [1] "float"

Mismatched surfaces chain. single call maps ONE codelist, winning codelist's terms (decodes) must line values — CDISC *N convention guarantees RACEN-style pairs, whose decodes character variable's submission values. two codelists share value surface (say SEXN's decodes "Female"/"Male" SEX holds "F"/"M"), unmatched values hit no_match policy; translate two hops instead — decode 's codelist first, to_code destination's:   Soft matches reported, never silent. title: Derive or translate a variable through its codelist — decode_column
code: spec <- artoo_spec(cdisc_sdtm_datasets, cdisc_sdtm_variables, codelists = cdisc_codelists)  # ---- Example 1: decode a coded variable into a display column ---- # # SEX is coded against C66731; map the codes to their decodes in a new # column, leaving the submission values untouched. dm <- decode_column(cdisc_dm, spec, "DM", from = "SEX", to = "SEXDECD") table(dm$SEX, dm$SEXDECD) #>     #>     Female Male #>   F     29    0 #>   M      0   31  # ---- Example 2: the RACEN pattern, a coded numeric from its decode ---- # # Declare SEXN as an integer variable owning a numeric codelist, then # derive it from SEX's decoded values: to_code maps each decode to its # submission code, and the spec dataType makes the result integer. vars <- rbind(   cdisc_sdtm_variables,   data.frame(     dataset = "DM", variable = "SEXN", label = "Sex (N)",     data_type = "integer", length = 8L, order = NA_integer_,     codelist_id = "SEXN"   ) ) cls <- rbind(   cdisc_codelists,   data.frame(     codelist_id = "SEXN", term = c("1", "2"),     decode = c("F", "M"), order = 1:2   ) ) spec_n <- artoo_spec(cdisc_sdtm_datasets, vars, codelists = cls) dm_n <- decode_column(cdisc_dm, spec_n, "DM",   from = "SEX", to = "SEXN", direction = "to_code" ) str(dm_n$SEXN) #>  int [1:60] 1 2 2 2 1 1 1 2 1 2 ... #>  - attr(*, "label")= chr "Sex (N)"

text: validated artoo_spec. Inspect spec_datasets() / spec_variables(), check validate_spec(), persist write_spec().

title: Read a specification from JSON, Excel, or Define-XML — read_spec
text: Three formats, one validator. .json file read artoo native JSON; .xlsx / .xls file read P21 workbook; .xml file read Define-XML 2.x. Either way result built artoo_spec(), type canonicalisation cross-slot integrity checks identical regardless source. Define-XML ingestion (needs xml2 package). ItemGroupDefs become datasets (keys derived ItemRef KeySequence), ItemRef + ItemDef pairs become variables, CodeLists become codelists (def:ExtendedValue = "Yes" marks extended term), MethodDefs / CommentDefs / leaves become supporting slots, ValueListDefs land value-level slot -clauses rendered readable text. Note: ExternalCodeList (MedDRA, ISO-3166) names dictionary, enumerable membership list; dropped, variables referenced carry codelist. Define-XML v1.0 (2005 model) refused guidance. P21 ingestion. Sheets located tolerant alias match (case-, space-, spelling-variant insensitive). Datasets Variables required; Codelists ValueLevel optional (latter becomes spec's value-level slot). Every cell read text, dataset codelist foreign keys forward-filled recover merged cells (Excel reader returns NA continuation rows). key resolved aborts artoo_error_spec rather silently dropped.

title: Read a specification from JSON, Excel, or Define-XML — read_spec
code: # ---- Example 1: round-trip a spec through native JSON ---- # # write_spec() and read_spec() are inverses on the JSON path: the spec # that comes back is identical to the one written. spec <- artoo_spec(cdisc_sdtm_datasets, cdisc_sdtm_variables, codelists = cdisc_codelists) path <- tempfile(fileext = ".json") write_spec(spec, path) back <- read_spec(path) identical(back, spec) #> [1] TRUE  # ---- Example 2: scope the read to one dataset ---- # # `datasets =` reads just the domain you are working on — validation is # scoped with it, so a problem elsewhere in the workbook cannot block # this dataset. dm_spec <- read_spec(path, datasets = "DM") spec_datasets(dm_spec) #> [1] "DM" head(spec_variables(dm_spec, "DM")[, c("variable", "label", "data_type")]) #>   variable                             label data_type #> 1  STUDYID                  Study Identifier    string #> 2   DOMAIN               Domain Abbreviation    string #> 3  USUBJID         Unique Subject Identifier    string #> 4   SUBJID  Subject Identifier for the Study    string #> 5  RFSTDTC Subject Reference Start Date/Time    string #> 6  RFENDTC   Subject Reference End Date/Time    string

title: Read a dataset from SAS XPORT — read_xpt
code: spec <- artoo_spec(   cdisc_adam_datasets, cdisc_adam_variables,   codelists = cdisc_codelists )  # ---- Example 1: round-trip a conformed dataset through xpt ---- # # Write ADSL, read it back; the variable labels and lengths survive. adsl <- apply_spec(cdisc_adsl, spec, "ADSL", conformance = "off") path <- tempfile(fileext = ".xpt") write_xpt(adsl, path) back <- read_xpt(path) get_meta(back)@columns$STUDYID$label #> [1] "Study Identifier"  # ---- Example 2: pick one member of a multi-member transport file ---- # # Build a two-member file by concatenating two single-member files (every # member section is 80-byte padded), then read one dataset out of it. dm <- apply_spec(cdisc_dm, sdtm_spec, "DM", conformance = "off") #> 1 variable the spec declares is absent from the data (not added): #> `BRTHDTC`. p_dm <- tempfile(fileext = ".xpt") write_xpt(dm, p_dm) #> Warning: Widened 1 column past the declared spec length: "STUDYID (7 -> 12)". #> ℹ Values need more bytes than the spec length; data was kept whole. #> ℹ Update the spec length, or shorten the data, so the file matches its #>   declared metadata. multi <- tempfile(fileext = ".xpt") writeBin(   c(     readBin(path, "raw", file.size(path)),     readBin(p_dm, "raw", file.size(p_dm))[-(1:240)]   ),   multi ) xpt_members(multi)$name #> [1] "ADSL" "DM"   nrow(read_xpt(multi, member = "DM")) #> [1] 60

title: Repair a spec from its conformance findings — repair_spec
code: # ---- Example 1: auto-repair an integer/fractional mismatch ---- # # adam_spec types ADSL.AGE as integer. Give it fractional ages and # check_spec() raises an integer_fraction error; repair_spec() flips AGE # (and only AGE) to float, and the corrected spec then applies cleanly. dat <- cdisc_adsl dat$AGE <- dat$AGE + 0.5 findings <- check_spec(dat, adam_spec, "ADSL") fixed <- repair_spec(adam_spec, findings) spec_variables(fixed, "ADSL")$data_type[   spec_variables(fixed, "ADSL")$variable == "AGE" ] #> [1] "float"  # ---- Example 2: nothing to repair is a no-op ---- # # The bundled data conforms, so its findings carry no integer_fraction or # integer_overflow rows and the spec is returned unchanged. clean <- check_spec(cdisc_adsl, adam_spec, "ADSL") identical(repair_spec(adam_spec, clean), adam_spec) #> No "integer_fraction" or "integer_overflow" findings to repair. #> ℹ The spec is returned unchanged. #> [1] TRUE

title: Override a variable's dataType in a spec — set_type
code: # ---- Example 1: retype one variable the data disagrees with ---- # # The bundled adam_spec types ADSL.AGE as integer. If an extract stored it # with fractional values, retype it to float so apply_spec() coerces # without loss. set_type() returns a new spec; the original is untouched. fixed <- set_type(adam_spec, "ADSL", AGE = "float") v <- spec_variables(fixed, "ADSL") v[v$variable == "AGE", c("variable", "data_type")] #>    variable data_type #> 16      AGE     float  # ---- Example 2: retype several at once, original left intact ---- # # Pass any number of variable = type pairs; canonical dataTypes and common # spellings both resolve. The source spec is immutable, so adam_spec still # reports AGE as its original type. patched <- set_type(adam_spec, "ADSL", AGE = "decimal", TRTSDT = "date") spec_variables(adam_spec, "ADSL")$data_type[   spec_variables(adam_spec, "ADSL")$variable == "AGE" ] #> [1] "integer"

title: Re-align metadata with a transformed data frame — sync_meta
code: spec <- artoo_spec(cdisc_adam_datasets, cdisc_adam_variables, codelists = cdisc_codelists)  # ---- Example 1: re-attach after an attribute-dropping subset ---- # # Base subsetting drops the metadata; capture it first, transform, then # sync. The metadata narrows to the kept columns and the new row count. adsl <- apply_spec(cdisc_adsl, spec, "ADSL", conformance = "off") meta <- get_meta(adsl) elderly <- adsl[adsl$AGE > 65, c("STUDYID", "USUBJID", "AGE")] synced <- sync_meta(elderly, meta) get_meta(synced)@dataset$records #> [1] 46  # ---- Example 2: a derived column gains a synthesized entry ---- # # A new column the metadata does not describe is profiled from its class, # so the frame still writes losslessly. adsl$AGEGR9 <- ifelse(adsl$AGE > 65, ">65", "<=65") synced2 <- sync_meta(adsl) #> Synthesized metadata for 1 new column: `AGEGR9`. #> ℹ Edit the spec (or the meta) if the inferred types need refining. get_meta(synced2)@columns$AGEGR9$dataType #> [1] "string"

title: Validate a specification for submission-readiness — validate_spec
code: # ---- Example 1: validate one dataset ---- # # Build a spec from the bundled ADaM tables and validate it; the # result prints a sectioned report and keeps the findings table. spec <- artoo_spec(   cdisc_adam_datasets, cdisc_adam_variables,   codelists = cdisc_codelists ) chk <- validate_spec(spec, dataset = "ADSL") chk@findings #>                check dimension severity dataset variable #> 1 study_name_present     study  warning    <NA>     <NA> #>                                               message #> 1 No study-level metadata; the study name is unknown.  # ---- Example 2: gate on errors with on_error = "abort" ---- # # Point a key at a missing variable, then validate with on_error = "abort" # and catch the resulting error. bad_ds <- cdisc_sdtm_datasets bad_ds$keys <- "NOTAVAR" bad <- artoo_spec(bad_ds, cdisc_sdtm_variables, codelists = cdisc_codelists) tryCatch(   validate_spec(bad, dataset = "DM", on_error = "abort"),   artoo_error_validation = function(e) conditionMessage(e)[1] ) #> [1] "\033[1m\033[22mSpec is not submission-ready, 1 error-severity finding.\n\033[31m✖\033[39m Dataset 'DM' keys reference variables not in the spec: NOTAVAR.\n\033[36mℹ\033[39m Inspect every finding in the returned artoo_check."

text: x dataset write. <data.frame>: required. Typically output apply_spec(), carrying artoo_meta. path Destination .json path. <character(1)>: required. on_invalid Policy values valid UTF-8. <character(1)>: default "error". One "error" (abort artoo_error_codec, naming offenders invalid bytes hex-escaped), "replace" (substitute ? warn artoo_warning_encoding), "ignore" (drop invalid bytes), "translit" / "fold" (like "error"; byte-level invalidity character fold, options exist one policy value can thread whole multi-format pipeline). policy vocabulary write_xpt(); text correctly read artoo always valid UTF-8, fires bytes entered frame mis-declared source encoding. created Creation timestamp. <POSIXct(1)> | NULL. NULL (default) stamps current time datasetJSONCreationDateTime; freeze byte-stable output. strict Suppress _artoo extension block. <logical(1)>: default FALSE. default file carries single namespaced _artoo object () content closed Dataset-JSON vocabulary express: SAS special-missing tags (.-.Z, ._), recorded source encoding, per-variable informats, origins, codelist references, significant digits. columns array always uses schema-legal keys. Data values stay plain nulls either way, foreign reader degrades gracefully. Note: strict = TRUE writes pure closed-vocabulary file validates official CDISC schema, warns (artoo_warning_codec) naming exactly dropped; attributes survive read-back.

title: Write a dataset to CDISC Dataset-JSON — write_json
text: Full metadata, loss. Unlike .xpt, .json file records complete artoo_meta: keySequence, codelist, origin, targetDataType, significantDigits survive. Dates, datetimes, times exchanged ISO 8601 strings, SAS-epoch numbers targetDataType "integer" (ADaM numeric-date convention); decimal rides string exact precision preserved. file always UTF-8 (RFC 8259 / CDISC v1.1). NaN infinite values valid CDISC numerics abort write. Streaming write, whole-file read. writer streams rows array bounded slabs (.json.gz path gzips stream transparently), read_json() must parse whole array . multi-million-row datasets prefer NDJSON variant (write_ndjson() / read_ndjson()), bounds memory directions.

title: Write a dataset to CDISC Dataset-JSON — write_json
code: # ---- Example 1: write a conformed dataset as Dataset-JSON ---- # # apply_spec() attaches the metadata; write_json() serializes the full # itemGroup plus the data rows. adsl <- apply_spec(cdisc_adsl, adam_spec, "ADSL", conformance = "off") #> 6 variables the spec declares are absent from the data (not added): #> `TRTDURD`, `DISONDT`, `EOSSTT`, `DCSREAS`, `EOSDISP`, and `MMS1TSBL`. path <- tempfile(fileext = ".json") write_json(adsl, path)  # ---- Example 2: a frozen timestamp for reproducible bytes ---- # # Fixing `created` makes two writes byte-identical; the columns() pane on # the written file shows the full metadata the file carries (DM is SDTM, # so it conforms against the bundled sdtm_spec). dm <- apply_spec(cdisc_dm, sdtm_spec, "DM", conformance = "off") #> 1 variable the spec declares is absent from the data (not added): #> `BRTHDTC`. path2 <- tempfile(fileext = ".json") write_json(dm, path2, created = as.POSIXct("2020-01-01", tz = "UTC")) columns(path2) #> <artoo_columns> DM -- 25 variables, 60 obs #> #   Variable  Type  Len  Format  Label                              Key #> 1   STUDYID   Char  7            Study Identifier                   1 #> 2   DOMAIN    Char  2            Domain Abbreviation #> 3   USUBJID   Char  14           Unique Subject Identifier          2 #> 4   SUBJID    Char  6            Subject Identifier for the Study #> 5   RFSTDTC   Char  10           Subject Reference Start Date/Time #> 6   RFENDTC   Char  10           Subject Reference End Date/Time #> 7   SITEID    Char  3            Study Site Identifier #> 8   AGE       Num                Age #> 9   AGEU      Char  5            Age Units #> 10  SEX       Char  16           Sex #> 11  RACE      Char  41           Race #> 12  ETHNIC    Char  22           Ethnicity #> 13  ARMCD     Char  8            Planned Arm Code #> 14  ARM       Char  20           Description of Planned Arm #> 15  COUNTRY   Char  3            Country #> 16  RFXSTDTC  Char  10 #> 17  RFXENDTC  Char  10 #> 18  RFICDTC   Char  1 #> 19  RFPENDTC  Char  16 #> 20  DTHDTC    Char  10 #> 21  DTHFL     Char  1 #> 22  ACTARMCD  Char  8 #> 23  ACTARM    Char  20 #> 24  DMDTC     Char  10 #> 25  DMDY      Num

text: x dataset write. <data.frame>: required. Typically output apply_spec(), carrying artoo_meta. path Destination .parquet path. <character(1)>: required. encoding Source charset record. <character(1)> | NULL. parquet bytes always written UTF-8 (format's STRING type UTF-8 spec); encoding records data's original charset artoo_meta, later write_xpt() can reproduce source bytes. NULL (default) leaves recorded encoding untouched. Tip: SAS IANA spelling listed artoo_encodings() accepted. on_invalid Policy values valid UTF-8. <character(1)>: default "error". One "error" (abort artoo_error_codec), "replace" (substitute ? warn artoo_warning_encoding), "ignore" (drop invalid bytes), "translit" / "fold" (accepted pipeline symmetry; behave "error" , since byte-level invalidity character fold). See write_json() fires; parquet STRING bytes UTF-8 spec, exactly like Dataset-JSON. compression Column compression codec. <character(1)>: default "snappy". One : "snappy" (default) — fast, parquet ecosystem default. "gzip" — smaller files, slower. "zstd" — best size/speed trade-supported. "uncompressed" — raw pages.

title: Write a specification to native JSON or a P21 Excel workbook — write_spec
text: spec specification serialise. <artoo_spec>: required. Build one artoo_spec() read_spec(). path Destination file. <character(1)>: required. extension picks format: .json (native, lossless) .xlsx (P21 interchange; needs writexl package). extension aborts artoo_error_input.

title: Write a specification to native JSON or a P21 Excel workbook — write_spec
text: output path, invisibly. Read back read_spec().

title: Write a specification to native JSON or a P21 Excel workbook — write_spec
text: Native JSON lossless format. slot written array row objects, NA encoded JSON null numbers full precision, read_spec() rebuilds identical artoo_spec artoo_spec(). Object keys emitted fixed order, writing spec twice yields byte-identical output. P21 xlsx interchange format. Sheets emitted headers P21 reader recognises (Define, Datasets, Variables, ValueLevel, Codelists, Methods, Comments, Documents; empty optional sheets omitted), foreign keys repeated every row (merged cells), spec's spec_standard() Datasets sheet's Standard column. study row writes back Define sheet's Attribute/Value pairs (StudyName, StudyDescription, ProtocolName). Data Type column written Define-XML / ODM vocabulary workbook expects: character variable text (Dataset-JSON string), decimal / double collapse float, boolean / URI text. Columns P21 vocabulary model lost: foreign column carried slot re-emitted verbatim header, xlsx round-trip keeps user columns. Note: fields P21 column (itemoid, target_data_type, per-variable key_sequence) survive xlsx round-trip; persist JSON need spec back exactly. Data Type re-encoding also non-injective: decimal, double, boolean, URI fold float text read-back. Define-XML partialDate / partialDatetime (partial / incomplete subtypes) read base date / datetime – CDISC Dataset-JSON v1.1 partial dataType – written back base type.

title: Write a specification to native JSON or a P21 Excel workbook — write_spec
code: # ---- Example 1: persist a spec to JSON, then read it back ---- # # Build a spec from the bundled CDISC-pilot tables, write it to a temp # JSON file, and confirm read_spec() reconstructs it intact. spec <- artoo_spec(   cdisc_adam_datasets, cdisc_adam_variables,   codelists = cdisc_codelists ) path <- tempfile(fileext = ".json") write_spec(spec, path) identical(read_spec(path), spec) #> [1] TRUE  # ---- Example 2: the same spec as a P21 workbook ---- # # The .xlsx path emits P21-shaped sheets; reading the workbook back # recovers the P21-representable surface (here: the dataset names). if (requireNamespace("writexl", quietly = TRUE)) {   xlsx <- tempfile(fileext = ".xlsx")   write_spec(spec, xlsx)   spec_datasets(read_spec(xlsx)) } #> [1] "ADSL"

text: Serialize data frame SAS Transport (.xpt) file v5 (FDA submission standard) v8 (extended names labels), preserving artoo_meta column can hold. emit end artoo workflow (spec -> apply_spec -> write_xpt); thin wrapper write_dataset() format = "xpt".

title: Write a dataset to SAS XPORT — write_xpt
code: write_xpt(   x,   path,   version = 5,   encoding = NULL,   on_invalid = c("error", "translit", "fold", "replace", "ignore"),   created = NULL )

title: Write a dataset to SAS XPORT — write_xpt
text: x dataset write. <data.frame>: required. Typically output apply_spec(), carrying artoo_meta. path Destination .xpt path. <character(1)>: required. version XPORT transport version. <integer(1)>: default 5. 5 (FDA standard: names <= 8 characters, labels <= 40 bytes) 8 (names <= 32, long labels). encoding Target charset. <character(1)> | NULL. NULL (default) inherits source encoding recorded artoo_meta, else UTF-8. IANA SAS names ("US-ASCII", "wlatin1") work. Tip: SAS IANA spelling listed artoo_encodings() accepted. on_invalid Policy values representable encoding. <character(1)>: default "error". policy vocabulary UTF-8 writers (write_json(), write_ndjson(), write_parquet()): "error" (default) — abort artoo_error_codec, naming offenders. "translit" — fold smart punctuation (curly quotes, en/em dashes, ellipsis, bullet) exact ASCII form per SAS NLS punctuation table warn; character fold (diacritic) still aborts. "fold" — "translit" plus ICU Latin-ASCII accent strip (Ö O, ß ss, Æ AE), warn. Lossy names — original characters recoverable; character neither table maps (Euro sign) still aborts. "replace" — substitute one ? per unrepresentable character warn artoo_warning_encoding. "ignore" — drop unrepresentable characters silently. Tip: US-ASCII submission write, "translit" fixes word-processor punctuation dominates real findings keeping genuine data corruption loud; reach "fold" accent stripping accepted, documented step migration. created Header timestamp. <POSIXct(1)> | NULL. NULL (default) stamps current time; freeze byte-stable output.

title: Write a dataset to SAS XPORT — write_xpt
text: XPORT can carry. .xpt file's NAMESTR stores variable name, label, length, SAS format. CDISC metadata beyond (keySequence, codelist, origin, targetDataType, ...) source encoding representable bytes; ride -session artoo_meta sidecar self-describing formats (Dataset-JSON, Parquet, rds). XPORT also distinguish empty string NA (store blanks) drops trailing spaces. Character ISO dates (--DTC) write text. character column whose dataType date/datetime/time numeric targetDataType CDISC ISO 8601 text form — SDTM --DTC convention — stores character variable, partial dates ("1951", "1951-12") included, byte byte. SAS-numeric encoding (DATE9.-style formats) used columns R Date/POSIXct/hms whose metadata records targetDataType = "integer" (ADaM numeric-date convention). character column targetDataType = "integer" aborts loudly — partial date can never become SAS numeric silently.

title: Write a dataset to SAS XPORT — write_xpt
code: spec <- artoo_spec(   cdisc_adam_datasets, cdisc_adam_variables,   codelists = cdisc_codelists )  # ---- Example 1: write a conformed dataset as v5 (FDA standard) ---- # # apply_spec() attaches the metadata; write_xpt() carries the label, length, # and SAS format for each variable into the transport file. adsl <- apply_spec(cdisc_adsl, spec, "ADSL", conformance = "off") path <- tempfile(fileext = ".xpt") write_xpt(adsl, path)  # ---- Example 2: v8 for long names, with a frozen timestamp ---- # # Version 8 keeps names over 8 characters; a fixed `created` makes the bytes # reproducible. Reading it back shows the labels, types, and record count # survived the transport. DM is SDTM, so it conforms against the bundled # sdtm_spec. dm <- apply_spec(cdisc_dm, sdtm_spec, "DM", conformance = "off") #> 1 variable the spec declares is absent from the data (not added): #> `BRTHDTC`. path8 <- tempfile(fileext = ".xpt") write_xpt(dm, path8, version = 8, created = as.POSIXct("2020-01-01", tz = "UTC")) #> Warning: Widened 1 column past the declared spec length: "STUDYID (7 -> 12)". #> ℹ Values need more bytes than the spec length; data was kept whole. #> ℹ Update the spec length, or shorten the data, so the file matches its #>   declared metadata. get_meta(read_xpt(path8))@dataset$records #> [1] 60

text: v5 recorded row count. v8 member records rows; v5 member's count derived byte span next member (end file) minus trailing padding, -character v5 member whose last row entirely blank reports one row fewer (documented v5 ambiguity, see write_xpt()).

title: List the members of a SAS XPORT transport file — xpt_members
code: spec <- artoo_spec(   cdisc_adam_datasets, cdisc_adam_variables,   codelists = cdisc_codelists )  # ---- Example 1: a single-member file reports one row ---- # # The FDA convention is one dataset per transport file. dm <- apply_spec(cdisc_dm, sdtm_spec, "DM", conformance = "off") #> 1 variable the spec declares is absent from the data (not added): #> `BRTHDTC`. p <- tempfile(fileext = ".xpt") write_xpt(dm, p) #> Warning: Widened 1 column past the declared spec length: "STUDYID (7 -> 12)". #> ℹ Values need more bytes than the spec length; data was kept whole. #> ℹ Update the spec length, or shorten the data, so the file matches its #>   declared metadata. xpt_members(p) #>   member name        label nvars nobs #> 1      1   DM Demographics    25   60  # ---- Example 2: survey a multi-member file, then read one member ---- # # Concatenate two single-member files into one library and list it. adsl <- apply_spec(cdisc_adsl, spec, "ADSL", conformance = "off") p2 <- tempfile(fileext = ".xpt") write_xpt(adsl, p2) multi <- tempfile(fileext = ".xpt") writeBin(   c(     readBin(p, "raw", file.size(p)),     readBin(p2, "raw", file.size(p2))[-(1:240)]   ),   multi ) xpt_members(multi) #>   member name                          label nvars nobs #> 1      1   DM                   Demographics    25   60 #> 2      2 ADSL Subject-Level Analysis Dataset    48   60

title: artoo 0.1.3
text: CRAN release: 2026-07-22 artoo_checks() gained invalid_encoding dimension (default): check_spec() flags character values whose bytes valid UTF-8, signature source read mis-declared encoding, writer aborts . artoo_encodings() name resolution now accepts SAS OEM/DOS encoding names (pcoem437, pcoem850, pcoem852, pcoem858, pcoem862, pcoem866, msdos737), reference table lists PCOEM437 / PCOEM850 rows. write_xpt(), write_json(), write_ndjson(), write_parquet() accept on_invalid = "translit", folding smart punctuation (curly quotes, en/em dashes, ellipsis, bullet) exact ASCII form per SAS NLS punctuation table; characters fold still abort loudly. write_xpt(), write_json(), write_ndjson(), write_parquet() also accept on_invalid = "fold": punctuation fold plus ICU Latin-ASCII accent strip (Ö O, ß ss, Æ AE), pinned data result identical every platform; characters neither table maps (Euro sign) still abort. write_xpt() now warns (artoo_warning_encoding) value forces column wider spec-declared length, instead widening silently; data still never truncated. write_xpt() writers’ on_invalid = "replace" now substitutes one ? per unrepresentable character instead one per byte (curly quote previously became ???). New article: Migrating clinical data WLATIN1 UTF-8, including smart-punctuation fold table byte-length migration recipe.

title: artoo 0.1.1
text: CRAN release: 2026-06-24 Initial CRAN release. artoo lightweight, lossless, CDISC-native reader writer clinical-trial datasets, built around one canonical metadata model (artoo_meta) conversion two supported formats lossless construction. Pure R lightweight, external SAS Java runtime.

title: artoo 0.1.1
text: Reads writes SAS XPORT (v5 v8), CDISC Dataset-JSON v1.1, NDJSON, Apache Parquet, RDS. Every codec carries full artoo_meta — labels, CDISC data types, lengths, SAS display formats, controlled-terminology references, sort keys — --conversion preserves complete metadata. Parquet metadata rides metadata_json sidecar; file written another tool sidecar degrades gracefully bare frame rather error. Generic read_dataset() / write_dataset() dispatch file extension, read_xpt() / write_xpt() matching read_json(), read_ndjson(), read_parquet(), read_rds() pairs direct entry points. Cross-cutting encoding, checks, created arguments flow .... Partial reads (col_select, n_max) every reader; gzip-transparent JSON NDJSON; multi-member SAS XPORT libraries via xpt_members() plus read_xpt(member = ). Numeric fidelity exact end end: decimal value exchanged string IEEE round-trip precision, integer values beyond R’s 32-bit range stay numeric rather overflowing, NaN / infinite values rejected invalid CDISC numerics. Rows sorted C-locale (byte) order, written file deterministic across locales matches SAS PROC SORT ASCII keys. Encodings follow IANA SAS standards: readers writers accept charset name either SAS R spelling (see artoo_encodings()), character columns transcoded UTF-8 NFC-normalized read, on_invalid = c("error", "replace", "ignore") policy governs invalid bytes uniformly across every writer.

title: artoo 0.1.1
text: artoo_spec() builds canonical metadata model Pinnacle 21 Excel workbook, Define-XML 2.0 / 2.1 file, native artoo JSON spec. read_spec() / write_spec() dispatch file extension: .xlsx writes Pinnacle 21 workbook (Define-XML P21 one composition), native JSON form lossless interchange round-trips spec identically. spec single-standard construction: @standard resolved explicit argument source, study-level fields canonicalized CDISC ODM vocabulary. Accessors include spec_standard(), spec_variables(), spec_codelists(), spec_methods(), spec_comments(). set_type() returns spec one variables retyped CDISC vocabulary; repair_spec() retypes every variable check_spec() run flags fractional --range integer data type, frame original spec refuse coerces one call.

title: artoo 0.1.1
text: apply_spec(x, spec, dataset, conformance = , na_position = ) coerces column CDISC data type, orders columns sorts rows spec’s keys, stamps artoo_meta. extra = c("keep", "drop") controls whether undeclared columns survive; on_coercion_loss = c("error", "keep") governs coercion lose data. pipeline never silently fabricates drops column: undeclared column reported kept, declared--absent column reported left absent. check_spec() validates data frame spec across conformance dimensions toggled artoo_checks(); check_study() runs whole study returns one stacked findings frame; conformance() reads findings back stamped frame. validate_spec() checks spec internal consistency bundled rule catalog, external dependency. decode_column() translates coded values codelist decodes; sync_meta() reconciles stamped frame’s metadata manual edits.
